name: Marble Caves of Chile Chico
description: Mother Nature outdoes herself with this stunningly beautiful set of caves carved into marble.

name: Montaña Mágica Lodge
description: This odd Chilean waterfall-cano hotel must be entered via a rope bridge.

name: ‘El Gigante’ and the Moai of Rapa Nui
description: The largest stone moai on Rapa Nui.

name: Termas Geometricas
description: This Japanese-inspired labyrinth of hot springs is hidden in a Chilean forest.

name: Hand of the Desert
description: A sculpture of a giant hand reaches for the stars in the middle of the Atacama desert.

name: Parque Quinta Normal Greenhouse
description: A beautiful deserted greenhouse in the middle of a park.

name: Punta Pite
description: A fantastical group of cliffside stairways, bridges, and twisting paths carved out of stone provide a surreal coastal hike.

name: World's Largest Swimming Pool
description: A record-breaking pool that uses computers to turn ocean water into cleaner, private swimming water.

name: Elevators of Valparaiso
description: What was once a unique system of urban elevators is turning into a mechanical ruin.

name: Orongo
description: This was once the center of the Birdman cult, and is now a World Heritage Site.

name: Sewell Ghost Town
description: The “City of Stairs” sits high in the Andes, above the world’s largest underground copper mine.

name: Caves of the Villarrica Volcano
description: One of Chile's most active volcanoes is riddled with beautiful caves.

name: Atacama Giant
description: One of the largest ancient geoglyphs in the world is a tall desert god used for predicting the weather.

name: Bahá’í Temple of South America
description: A fantastical and futuristic temple of light built from nine monumental glass veils.

name: The VLT - Very Large Telescope
description: In the most arid desert on the planet, the world's leading telescope allows astronomers to see more than has ever been seen before.

name: Ana Kai Tangata (Cannibal Cave)
description: The ominously named cave is home to some remarkable ancient art.

name: El Tatio Geysers
description: The largest geyser field in the southern hemisphere is a spectacular sight.

name: Hotel Nothofagus
description: This enchanting rainforest hotel looks as though it grew from the forest itself.

name: Lagunas Escondidas de Baltinache (Hidden Lagoons of Baltinache)
description: You can swim in these highly salty, mesmerizingly blue lakes in the Atacama Desert.

name: Museum of Memory and Human Rights
description: A museum founded to reflect on and remember the human rights abuses under Chilean dictator Augusto Pinochet.

name: The Navel of the World
description: This large round boulder is said to emit spiritual power, but it may just be magnets.

name: Museo a Cielo Abierto
description: The gorgeous murals at this open-air museum form the largest street art collective in Chile.

name: Robinson Crusoe Island
description: Named for the DeFoe novel inspired by a stranded sailor, this island is known for its rare plants and extreme beauty.

name: The Nitrate Towns of Chile
description: Towns with a resource known as "white gold," valuable enough to fight a war over, now stand ghostly and empty.

name: The Devil's Blast
description: The hellish conditions of this abandoned mine that snakes beneath the Pacific Ocean gave it its diabolic name.

name: National Astronomical Observatory of Chile
description: This observatory high on a hilltop in the center of Santiago got its start in 1852.

name: Iglesia de San Francisco
description: This iconic hilltop church doubled as a lighthouse in the 19th century, guiding ships into the port of Valparaíso.

state: Región de Magallanes y de la Antártica Chilena
name: Shipwreck of the Lord Lonsdale
description: The rusty hull of this 1899 ship serves as a tribute to explorers of the bottom of the world.

name: Pukará de Quitor
description: The ruins of a pre-Columbian fortress city so secure it held off the Spanish conquistadors for over 20 years.

name: Atacama Large Millimeter Array (ALMA)
description: One of the world's most advanced radio observatories sprawls across the Atacama Desert.

name: Dock of Souls (Muelle de las Almas)
description: A wooden dock art installation on Chiloé Island taps into the island's indigenous mythology.

name: Librería del Desierto
description: A beautiful little bookshop located in the middle of a desert.

name: Enrique Molina Theater
description: The ruins of a grand theater destroyed during the most powerful seismic catastrophe ever recorded.

name: La Chascona
description: Pablo Neruda's custom-built lover's nest was made to remind him of the sea and named after his mistress' hair.

name: Mataveri International Airport
description: The world's most remote airport.

name: Museo Chileno de Arte Precolombino (Chilean Museum of Pre-Columbian Art)
description: This museum covers 10,000 years of Pre-Columbian Latin American history.

name: Foucault Pendulum of Valdivia
description: A 63-foot-tall swinging ball hypnotizes passersby, and proves to any remaining doubters that the Earth is spinning.

name: La Picá de Clinton
description: How a lunch counter in Chile became a shrine to the 42nd president of the United States

name: The Dissidents Cemetery
description: This historic Chilean cemetery is the burial place of hundreds of European and American freethinkers and non-Catholics.

name: Museo de Sitio (In Situ Museum)
description: Walk on a glass floor suspended over ancient mummified remains.

name: Monument to the Indigenous People
description: This sculpture meant to honor the bravery of the Indigenous Mapuche people of Chile proved controversial.

name: Pu o Hiro (Hiro's Trumpet)
description: In the Middle Ages, villagers would sound this stone trumpet to boost fertility and coax fish to shore.

name: Meteorite Museum (Museo del Meteorito)
description: A massive display of meteorites all found in the Atacama Desert.

state: XII Región de Magallanes y la Antártica Chilena
name: Port Famine
description: The geographical center of Chile is a port so depressing that it has driven men to suicide.

name: La  Sebastiana
description: Pivotal home of Nobel Prize winner Pablo Neruda.

name: Tinoco Tunnel
description: Locals visit this abandoned train tunnel where a man took his own life to ask favors of his spirit.

name: Pablo Fierro Museum
description: This fairytale house on the shore of a Chilean Lake oozes an eclectic mix of Chilote and German Cultural heritage.

state: Región de Magallanes y de la Antártica Chilena
name: Glacier Republic
description: An unrecognized micronation declared by Greenpeace to protect the glaciers of Chile.

name: Plaza Jurásica
description: Full-scale dinosaurs dominate the forested park in the middle of Chile’s second city.

name: Malleco Viaduct
description: This bright yellow bridge is a true engineering feat.

state: Región de Magallanes y de la Antártica Chilena
name: Nao Victoria Museum
description: A display of hand-built replicas of the legendary vessels used by Patagonian and Antarctic explorers.

name: Chacabuco
description: This abandoned nitrate town was repurposed as a dictator's prison.

name: Villa Baviera
description: Formerly a brutal Nazi colony, this Chilean village was remade as a (controversial) German-themed tourist trap.

name: Churches of Chiloé
description: Spanish religion mixed with local architecture has yielded a network of churches unlike any other.

name: Torre Telefonica Chile
description: It's just another skyscraper, but it's shaped like '90s cell phone.

name: Cementerio Numero 3 de Playa Ancha
description: Hand-made memorials and a wish-granting murderer lie within this seaside pauper's cemetery.

name: O'Brien Submarine Museum
description: A 300-foot-long Cold War submarine floats in a Chilean River as a maritime museum.

name: Botica Salcobrand
description: Buying your medicine at this particular pharmacy is like taking a trip back in time.

name: Yerbas Buenas Petroglyphs
description: At this beautifully preserved Pre-Hispanic site, you can see ancient petroglyphs with depictions of llamas and alpacas.

name: Wulff Castle
description: A whimsical European-style chateau that calls the coast of Chile home.

name: La Silla Observatory
description: The Chilean mountain is freckled with (relatively) small European telescopes.

name: 'Independent Republic' of Hualqui
description: Despite being of the most short-lived republics in history, this small Chilean town won’t forget its two days of independence.

name: German Colonial Museum
description: A sliver of 19th-century Germany sits on the shore of a Chilean lake.

name: Museo Mineralógico (Mineralogical Museum)
description: One of the world's most complete mineralogical collections.

state: Magallanes y la Antártica Chilena Region
name: Cape Horn Monument
description: This sculpture pays homage to sailors that perished attempting to round Cape Horn.

name: Kotepokouri Rano Kau
description: A volcanic crater that visitors can look inside.

state: XII Región de Magallanes y la Antártica Chilena
name: 'Sin Palabras' ('Speechless')
description: An hour south of Punta Arenas (Chile) along the Strait of Magellan, a Kcymaerxthaere plaque tells the story of Culev Larsze, cursed to live without words in the fictional world of Kcymaerxthaere.

name: 'The Presence of Latin America' Mural
description: The imposing piece of artwork is a celebration of Pan-American fraternity.

name: Entre Cordillera Restobar Solar
description: At this desert restaurant, meals are cooked in solar ovens.

name: Church of Guayacán
description: A charmingly unusual prefabricated metal church designed by Alexandre Gustave Eiffel.

name: World's Largest Coca-Cola Logo
description: 70,000 empty bottles of Coke comprise an ad seen only from the air.

name: Cementerio General de Santiago
description: The largest cemetery in Chile is the final resting place for more than 2 million people, from politicians to artists.

name: Willimapu Gastronomía Ancestral
description: In a corner of Santiago's largest market, this inventive Mapuche eatery shows "ancestral cuisine" doesn't have to mean old-fashioned.

name: Bismarck Tower
description: Dedicated to the first German Chancellor, Otto von Bismarck, this aging edifice is the only one of its kind in the Americas.

name: Reloj de Flores (Flower Clock)
description: Built for the 1962 World Cup, this fully functioning botanical clock is still ticking.

name: Punta Guaque 01
description: This hidden pre-Columbian rock art depicts a whale hunt.

name: Toconao Bell Tower
description: This 18th-century tower is locally iconic.

name: Museo de Historia Natural Río Seco
description: A gorgeous and extensive collaboration between biologists and artists, displaying skeletons and other animal remnants in lifelike poses.

name: 99 Restaurante
description: A rotating nine-course menu offers delicious deep-dives into the foodways of Chilean agricultural valleys.

name: Lego Atacama Large Millimeter Array
description: A surprisingly detailed model of the world's most powerful radio telescope.

name: The Copiapó
description: South America's oldest surviving locomotive.

name: James Bond Rocks
description: Giant fake rocks that shielded Bond lie in wait for their next action scene.

name: Antofagasta Mountainside Messages
description: Instead of using billboards, some people here relay their messages on the side of a mountain.

name: Kitson Meyer Locomotive
description: The antique vehicle is a preserved relic of Chile's nitrate mining industry.

name: Moai 263
description: A moai decorated with the depiction of a European ship.

name: AMAIA Restaurante
description: From maqui pisco sours to mote-mushroom risotto, the “Mapuchef” elevates endemic ingredients and celebrates Mapuche foodways.

name: Peulla
description: This secluded village in the Andean wilderness can't be accessed by road.

name: Statue of Juan Godoy
description: A monument of the man who spurred Copiapó's silver mining industry.

name: Centro Artesanal Los Dominicos
description: What was once rural farmland owned by the Dominican Order is now an artisanal crafts market enveloped by Santiago’s urban sprawl.

name: Cristo de Mayo
description: A wooden crucifix that survived a catastrophic earthquake, stared down a tyrannical aristocrat, and still draws Santiago’s faithful centuries later.
